hero.desc: I design intuitive digital products focusing on user needs, visual craft, and systems thinking — from initial sketch to launch.

items.title: UI/UX Design
items.desc: Designs focused on usability, visual hierarchy, and interaction craft; from wireframes to pixel-perfect interfaces.

items.title: Design Systems
items.desc: Scalable token architectures, component libraries, and design-to-code documentation enabling teams to work in harmony.

items.title: User Research
items.desc: Interviews, usability testing, card sorting, and synthesis frameworks to ground design decisions in real user behavior.

contact.title: Let's work
contact.body: Have a product challenge or design role in mind? I'm always open to discussing new opportunities and ideas.

about.lede: I am a Product Designer creating user-centered digital products since 2018. I independently designed the UI/UX of the RouteWise App, contributed to the design team of Akaunting, and delivered design solutions for the Ticaret Bakanlığı Free Zones Platform, TUSAŞ Hub, ASELSAN Hub, and EntekBir Mobile projects. All are live and in active use — built around usability, accessibility, and craft. Beyond UI/UX, I work in motion, branding, and graphic design.
about.ctaLede: Have a product challenge or role in mind? I'm always open to discussing new opportunities.

hireModal.title: Hire me
hireModal.lede: Tell me about your project or role and I'll get back within 6 hours.
hireModal.descriptionPlaceholder: A few sentences about the product, goals, and what kind of design help you're looking for…
hireModal.descriptionHint: What are you building, and what do you need?
hireModal.privacy: Your message goes straight to my inbox. No newsletters, no third parties.

items.title: StandardFlow Website UI Design
items.desc: End-to-end website UI design — landing page, marketing flow, and conversion-focused interactions for a SaaS product. Full case study covering visual system and key screens.
highlights: Full landing-page flow built on a tight visual system
Hero, features, social proof and pricing sections — conversion-focused layout
Reusable patterns and components, ready for handoff
Built in Figma with attention to typography and motion craft

items.title: Accounting Software UI Design
items.desc: Interface design and design-system work for an accounting SaaS — invoices, reports, and dashboard surfaces with a scalable component library underneath.
highlights: Interface design for invoices, reports, and dashboard surfaces
Component-library foundation supporting consistent product UI
Co-designed with a partner designer on layout and data patterns
Built in Figma; legacy XD/Illustrator artwork integrated where useful

items.title: Project Management App
items.desc: Product design for a project management tool — landing surface and key in-app screens, focused on clarity for cross-functional teams.
highlights: Marketing landing page paired with in-app product screens
Information hierarchy tuned for fast scanning across long task lists
Designed in Figma with consistent token and component usage

items.title: Pricing Page UI Design
items.desc: A focused pricing-page exploration — clear plan comparison, conversion-oriented hierarchy, and a small interaction system around toggles and CTAs.
highlights: Plan comparison tuned for fast scanning
Toggle interactions and supporting micro-states
Conversion-led visual hierarchy on primary CTA

items.title: Routewise UI Design
items.desc: This UI design focuses on making multi-vehicle tracking intuitive and effortless. It transforms complex data into a clean, user-friendly experience.
highlights: Restrained visual language for a high-trust category
Strong typographic hierarchy across hero and service sections
Designed in Figma with reusable section patterns

items.title: LogoType Design
items.desc: A collection of logotype and brand-identity explorations — wordmarks, lockups, and typographic studies across multiple brand contexts.
highlights: Wordmarks and lockups across multiple brand directions
Typographic exploration and custom letterform tuning
Crafted in Illustrator with supporting Photoshop work

lab.lede: A workshop of interactive experiments. Each piece is built in code — no static mockups — to explore a specific motion or interaction principle. Designed and engineered end-to-end as a place to play.

cursorSymphony.title: Cursor Symphony
cursorSymphony.blurb: A cursor that breathes — state-morphs over text, buttons, and media, with magnetic snapping driven by spring physics.

tokenForge.title: Token Forge
tokenForge.blurb: A live design-token playground. Drag sliders to tune accent hue, radius, and density — watch the system re-flow.

driftPhysics.title: Drift Physics
driftPhysics.blurb: Throw a card; it tumbles, settles, and snaps back. Real momentum, constrained drag, spring damping.

scrollCinema.title: Scroll Cinema
scrollCinema.blurb: A scroll-linked cinematic sequence. Text reveals, parallax depth layers, and color theme shifts mapped to scroll progress.

systemCreator.title: System Creator
systemCreator.blurb: A full design-system builder. Color, typography, radius, stroke — every choice paints a live UI kit you can copy out as CSS or JSON.

typeSymphony.title: Type Symphony
typeSymphony.blurb: A variable-font playground. Drag five axes — weight, width, slant, optical size, grade — or press play and watch the letters dance through an 8-second choreography.

cursorSymphony.lede: Move your pointer across the surfaces below. The cursor remembers what it's touching — text gets a caret, links open into a ring, media expands into a viewfinder, and magnetic anchors pull it home.
cursorSymphony.textBody: Long-form text invites a precise caret. Notice how the cursor tightens into a slim vertical line so you can still see exactly which letter you're grazing — like an editor caret, not a blob.
cursorSymphony.linkBody: The cursor opens into a 64px ring and labels its intent.
cursorSymphony.magnetsBody: Each pellet pulls the cursor toward its center.
cursorSymphony.footerNote: <0>useMotionValue</0> for raw pointer coords, <0>useSpring</0> for two-layer smoothing, <0>useTransform</0> for ambient hue mapping, and a small state machine driving variant-style animation transitions.

tokenForge.lede: A working design system, miniature. Drag the sliders to retune accent hue, radius, density, and weight — every component you see below is bound to those tokens and re-flows live.
tokenForge.body: Every token is wired. Push a value; watch the system breathe.
tokenForge.footerNote: A reactive token graph: five state values feed every visual property. <0>LayoutGroup</0> + <0>motion.div layout</0> animates dimension changes when radius and density shift, so the system reads as alive rather than jerky.

driftPhysics.lede: Grab a card and throw it. The drag transition tracks velocity, adds momentum, and lets cards skid against the walls before springing back to rest. Double-click to dismiss.
driftPhysics.footerNote: <0>drag</0> + <0>dragConstraints</0> against a container ref, custom <0>dragTransition</0> for inertia tuning, and <0>useTransform</0> mapping x velocity to rotation for a hand-thrown feel.

scrollCinema.lede: A scroll-linked cinematic sequence. Text reveals, parallax depth layers, and color theme shifts mapped to scroll progress.
scrollCinema.reducedLede: Reduced-motion mode is enabled — the cinematic sequence is disabled. Below is a static summary of what this experiment showcases.
scrollCinema.act1Body: Every interaction is a small commitment between system and user. Motion is how we deliver on it — confirming, guiding, rewarding.
scrollCinema.act2Body: Easing is gravity. A spring that overshoots feels alive; a linear curve feels like a robot. Choose the physics, choose the feeling.
scrollCinema.act3Body: Choreographed reveals scaffold attention. The right element arrives the moment it's asked for — never sooner.
scrollCinema.footerNote: A single <0>useScroll</0> source spring-smoothed, then <0>useTransform</0>ed into a dozen choreographed outputs: color theme, parallax depths, sequential reveals, and a final stamp. One scroll variable, an entire scene.

systemCreator.lede: A working design system, in full. Tune primary hue, font pairing, radius scale, and stroke width — the preview rebuilds itself live with a 10-step palette, type scale, and components ready to copy out.
systemCreator.typeBody: Body text carries the meaning. It should read effortlessly, breathe between lines, and let the system fade away.
systemCreator.cardBody: A modular dashboard with reusable patterns across surfaces.
systemCreator.footerNote: Every visual choice flows from a small graph of tokens: <0>primary hue</0> generates a 10-step palette, <0>type scale</0> drives every text size, <0>radius</0> and <0>stroke</0> shape every surface and outline. <0>LayoutGroup</0> animates the in-between as you re-tune.

typeSymphony.lede: Variable fonts let one font file behave like an entire family. Drag the five axes below to bend Roboto Flex through its full design space, or press play to watch the letters dance through a choreographed 8-second symphony.
typeSymphony.editAria: Type to change the sample text
typeSymphony.footerNote: Roboto Flex carries five active axes (<0>wght</0>, <0>wdth</0>, <0>slnt</0>, <0>opsz</0>, <0>GRAD</0>). Each slider writes directly into <0>font-variation-settings</0>. Press play and <0>animate()</0> from framer-motion drives a single 0→1 timeline that maps to a four-phase choreography across all axes simultaneously.
